title: Lightweight updates fail when a table has a MATERIALIZED column whose value depends on an EPHEMERAL column
description:
### Company or project name
n/a
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
Attempting to perform a lightweight update on a table with a `MATERIALIZED` column which depends on an `EPHEMERAL` column results in an error that the `EPHEMERAL` column does not exist. This is unexpected since the value of the `EPHEMERAL` column is not relevant for the update being performed.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
25.7
### How to reproduce
https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/8d3f4fc2-a3f3-47c9-b3e4-e394c1f33dd6
```
CREATE TABLE t1 (c1 String EPHEMERAL, c2 String MATERIALIZED tryBase64Decode(c1), c3 BOOLEAN)
ENGINE=MergeTree
ORDER BY tuple()
SETTINGS enable_block_number_column=1, enable_block_offset_column=1;
INSERT INTO t1(c1, c3) VALUES ('SGVsbG8gV29ybGQh', true);
SET asterisk_include_materialized_columns = 1;
SELECT * FROM t1;
SET allow_experimental_lightweight_update = 1;
UPDATE t1 SET c3 = false WHERE c2='Hello World!';
SELECT * FROM t1;
```
### Expected behavior
Lightweight update should complete without error.
### Error message and/or stacktrace
```
Received exception from server (version 25.7.1):
Code: 47. DB::Exception: Received from localhost:9000. DB::Exception: Missing columns: 'c1' while processing: 'tryBase64Decode(c1)', required columns: 'c1', available columns: '_block_number' '_row_exists' '_part_data_version' '_part_offset' '_disk_name' '_sample_factor' '_part_starting_offset' '_part_index' '_part' '_part_granule_offset' '_block_offset' '_part_uuid' 'c3' '_partition_id' 'c2'. (UNKNOWN_IDENTIFIER)
(query: UPDATE t1 SET c3 = false WHERE c2='Hello World!';)
```
### Additional context
Replacing the `MATERIALIZED` column with a `DEFAULT` column eliminates the error, but this changes the semantics of the column.
This appears to be the same failure mode as #68839 and #71793.
poc:
CREATE TABLE t1 (c1 String EPHEMERAL, c2 String MATERIALIZED tryBase64Decode(c1), c3 BOOLEAN)
ENGINE=MergeTree
ORDER BY tuple()
SETTINGS enable_block_number_column=1, enable_block_offset_column=1;
INSERT INTO t1(c1, c3) VALUES ('SGVsbG8gV29ybGQh', true);
UPDATE t1 SET c3 = false WHERE c2='Hello World!';
key_bug_features: Lightweight update fails with MATERIALIZED column depending on EPHEMERAL column
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2 (c1 String EPHEMERAL, c2 String MATERIALIZED tryBase64Decode(c1), c3 Int32, c4 Float64 DEFAULT 0.0) ENGINE=MergeTree ORDER BY tuple();
UPDATE t2 SET c3 = 0 WHERE c2 = 'Hello World!';
SELECT * FROM t2 WHERE c3 IS NOT NULL;
